subject: Math

lesson.story_mission: Join the Skill World design crew to use find area in square units and perimeter as distance around in real classroom missions.
lesson.mini_lesson: Find area in square units and perimeter as distance around.
lesson.objective: Find area in square units and perimeter as distance around.
lesson.concept_explanation: Use models and precise math language to find area in square units and perimeter as distance around. Explain the strategy and include units when the answer needs them.
lesson.common_misconception: Learners may use square units for perimeter or linear units for area.
lesson.real_world_connection: These skills help with schedules, materials, maps, art projects, and data from everyday activities.
lesson.lesson_summary: Read the task, choose the model, solve carefully, and check that the answer matches the question.
hints: Read what the question asks.
Use the visual model to organize the information.
Check that your answer includes the right unit or name.

misconception_bank.area_perimeter_confusion: Learners may use square units for perimeter or linear units for area.
misconception_bank.square_unit_count_error: Learners may skip or double-count squares in an array.
misconception_bank.side_length_addition_error: Learners may forget one side when adding around a figure.
misconception_bank.formula_misuse: Learners may multiply when they need to add, or add when they need to multiply.

game_theme.title: Skill World: Area and Perimeter

questions.prompt: For a 6 by 2 rectangle, find the perimeter.
hints: Read what the question asks.
Use the visual model to organize the information.
Check that your answer includes the right unit or name.

feedback.incorrect: Use the model and check the question again.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is 16 units.
questions.read_aloud_text: For a 6 by 2 rectangle, find the perimeter, or units around,.

questions.prompt: For a 7 by 4 rectangle, find the area.
hints: Read what the question asks.
Use the visual model to organize the information.
Check that your answer includes the right unit or name.

feedback.incorrect: Use the model and check the question again.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is 28 square units.
questions.read_aloud_text: For a 7 by 4 rectangle, find the area. Choices: 28 square units, 22 units, 11.

story.text: Listen to this page. This mission introduces area and perimeter. Look inside the shape for square units and around the outside for perimeter in the picture. Think about how the mission story connects to the math. Decide whether the question asks for inside space or units around as you get ready. Press Start Mission when you are ready.
story.label: Read This Page

lesson.text: This mini lesson teaches area and perimeter. Look inside the shape for square units and around the outside for perimeter and read the vocabulary words. Decide whether the question asks for inside space or units around one step at a time. Watch out for using area when the question asks for perimeter, or perimeter when it asks for area. Press Next to continue.
lesson.label: Read This Page

watch.text: This Watch page shows a worked example for Area and Perimeter. Look at the model and listen for how each part matches the question. Step one, read what is being asked. Step two, use the visual model. Step three, check the final answer with the right units or math words. Press Next to continue.
watch.label: Read This Page

demo.text: This guided demo lets you practice area and perimeter with help. Look inside the shape for square units and around the outside for perimeter before you choose an answer. Decide whether the question asks for inside space or units around and explain your thinking in math words. Do not rush past using area when the question asks for perimeter, or perimeter when it asks for area. Press Next to continue.
demo.label: Read This Page

practice.text: Practice time. Use the model to help you work on area and perimeter. Decide whether the question asks for inside space or units around and check your answer. Press Read Question if you want the exact question read aloud. Choose your answer when you are ready.
practice.label: Read This Page

challenge.text: Challenge time. The problem may need careful thinking about area and perimeter. Look inside the shape for square units and around the outside for perimeter, then decide which strategy fits best. Press Read Question if you want the exact question read aloud. Answer carefully, then continue.
challenge.label: Read This Page

checkpoint.text: Checkpoint time. Show what you know about area and perimeter. Read the problem, use the visual, and check for using area when the question asks for perimeter, or perimeter when it asks for area. Press Read Question if you want the exact question read aloud. Choose or write the answer when ready.
checkpoint.label: Read This Page

badge.text: This badge page celebrates your work on Area and Perimeter. Look at your stars and remember the strategy you used. Mistakes helped your brain grow when you checked them. Press Next to see your profile.
badge.label: Read This Page

profile.text: This profile page shows how your mission went. Look at your accuracy, hints, and growth notes. If a level needs more practice, go back to the hub or continue to Skill Practice. Use your results to choose what to practice next.
profile.label: Read This Page
